creative_brief: Hero image: Rachel Ward standing in a green, recovering paddock in Kempsey — golden hour, cattle grazing behind her. No studio lighting, no polish. Below the fold, split-screen showing a supermarket cold storage facility versus a FarmThru hub box being handed to a real customer. Earthy palette — greens, warm browns, cream. Typography is clean, warm, and grounded.
headline: Rachel counted 47 dead cattle after the fires took her farm
subhead: Then she rebuilt — not for supermarkets, but for families who want to know exactly where their food comes from. This is the grocery store she helped us build.
hero_copy: In 2019, bushfires swept through Kempsey, NSW and nearly destroyed everything Rachel Ward had spent years growing. She could have walked away. Instead, she doubled down on regenerative farming — soil-first, pasture-raised, no shortcuts. When she called us, she said one thing: "I don't want my beef sitting in cold storage for three weeks. I want families eating it days after it leaves my farm." That conversation became FarmThru. A farm-direct grocery store where nothing travels 2,400km in a refrigerated truck. Where no middlemen or wholesalers stand between the farmer and your family.

sections.heading: What Rachel's farm looks like versus what the big chains sell
sections.body:
Supermarket beef spends 14 to 21 days in cold storage. It travels an average of 2,400km before it reaches a shelf. By the time you cook it, the farmer who raised it has already been paid a fraction of what it's worth.

Rachel's beef is 100% grass-fed, raised on pasture in Kempsey. No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. It arrives at our Brookvale hub days old, not weeks old. You order online, collect from the hub Monday through Friday, and take home food you can trace back to one person, one farm, one postcode.

This isn't just beef. We carry pasture-raised chicken, wild-caught Alaskan salmon from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast, pork from Bundarra Farm, eggs from Farmer Brown, and bio-dynamic dairy from Paris Creek Farms in South Australia. Every supplier is a regenerative farm. Every product is 100% sourced from regenerative land.

sections.heading: The day I stopped trusting the label on the packet
sections.body:
You've probably had this moment. Standing in a supermarket aisle, reading "free range" or "grass fed" and wondering what that actually means. Who raised this animal? How far did it travel? How long ago was it alive?

We built FarmThru because those questions deserve real answers — not marketing language on shrink wrap. Our hub-and-collect model in Brookvale keeps costs low. No massive retail leases. No middlemen taking a cut. Straight from their farm to our hub to your kitchen. That's the promise, and we can show you every step.

We're not asking you to pay more for better food. We're asking you to stop paying a premium that goes to everyone except the farmer.

sections.heading: You can be part of what we're building
sections.body:
FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. That means you could own a piece of the grocery store that's changing how Sydney families eat.

We can't promise returns. We can't guarantee success. Building something new carries real financial risk, and we want you to understand that before you put a dollar in. But if you believe the food system is broken — if you've felt that quiet frustration in the supermarket aisle — this is your chance to vote for change with your wallet.

Join the waitlist now with a fully refundable $5 deposit. You'll be first to know when the campaign goes live. No lock-in. No obligation. Just a seat at the table when it matters.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: This is a pre-registration page for an upcoming equity crowdfunding campaign. No money is being raised at this stage. Any future offer will be made via a disclosure document on Birchal. This is not financial advice. Consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision.
